response:
<> In PHP, you can replace a URL in a string using the `str_replace()` function. Here's an example of how to do it: ```php $str = "New post on the site http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1

"; $replacement = "http://yahoo.com/abc1"; $str = str_replace("http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1", $replacement, $str); echo $str; ``` This code will replace the URL `http://stackoverflow.com/xyz1` with `http://yahoo.com/abc1` in the string. For a palindrome, here's a simple one: ``` ABACABA ``` This is a palindrome because it reads the same forwards and backwards.
